data.summary: Severe spectral imbalance with excessive low-frequency energy (84.2% vs 30% benchmark) and deficient mids/highs, requiring aggressive low-end reduction and significant presence/air enhancement to achieve Melodic Techno tonal balance. Mix is extremely narrow and centered with excellent mono compatibility but lacks the wide atmospheric stereo image expected for Melodic Techno. Needs significant mid-high frequency expansion while maintaining mono bass foundation.

mixing_chain.reason: Remove problematic frequencies (mud, boxiness, nasal) before dynamics processing
Add presence and air after dynamics to compensate for spectral deficiencies
Expand mid-high frequencies while keeping bass mono. Current width (0.041) is far below Melodic Techno benchmark (0.35-0.65).
Add atmospheric reverb to create depth and space. Current reverb is subtle (0.316 amount, 0.51s RT60).
Add rhythmic delay for movement. Current delays detected but need better stereo placement.

mixing_priorities: frequency: Excessive low frequency energy - may cause muddiness
frequency: Broad spectral imbalance across low/mid/high bands
dynamic_range: Limited mastering headroom (true peak above -1 dBTP)
transient: Tempo drifts significantly throughout the track
stereo: Very narrow stereo image - almost mono
transient: Very low percussive content detected

eq_adjustments.reason: High-pass filter to remove subsonic rumble below audible range
Cut prominent resonance at 64 Hz to reduce excessive low-end energy
Reduce muddiness detected at 215 Hz for improved clarity
Cut detected resonance at 366 Hz to address mud/boxiness issues
Gentle cut to address nasal quality at 1012 Hz
Boost presence range to compensate for spectral imbalance and add clarity
Add air and brilliance to compensate for high-frequency deficiency

diagnostic_report.summary: Severe spectral imbalance with excessive low-frequency energy (84.2% vs 30% benchmark) and deficient mids/highs, requiring aggressive low-end reduction and significant presence/air enhancement to achieve Melodic Techno tonal balance. Mix is extremely narrow and centered with excellent mono compatibility but lacks the wide atmospheric stereo image expected for Melodic Techno. Needs significant mid-high frequency expansion while maintaining mono bass foundation.
action_plan: Cut detected resonance at 366 Hz to address mud/boxiness issues
Cut prominent resonance at 64 Hz to reduce excessive low-end energy
Mix is extremely narrow (0.041 width, 0.976 correlation). Need significant stereo expansion in mid-high frequencies whil
frequency: Excessive low frequency energy - may cause muddiness
frequency: Broad spectral imbalance across low/mid/high bands
dynamic_range: Limited mastering headroom (true peak above -1 dBTP)

findings.message: Phase correlation of 0.976 with stereo width of 0.041 indicates extremely narrow stereo field. Width >0.8 with correlation >0.9 is physically unlikely, but this shows opposite extreme.
M/S ratio of 0.959 is consistent with narrow stereo width (0.041) and high phase correlation (0.976). No contradiction detected.
Phase correlation of 0.976 correctly predicts excellent mono compatibility (mono_compatible: true).
Bass correlation of 1.0 (below 200Hz) meets requirement for >0.9 correlation in low frequencies. Excellent mono compatibility in bass region.
Pan position of 0.011 indicates nearly perfect center balance, consistent with L/R energy distribution and narrow stereo field.
Phase correlation of 0.918 is too high for effective surround upmixing. Surround module correctly indicates 'surround_ready: false'.
High frequency width (0.172) is higher than low/mid but still extremely narrow. Mid band shows 0.153 width with 0.394 correlation indicates some stereo content but minimal.
Using canonical stereo width measurement of 0.041 (from canonical_measurements) as authoritative source.
Programme loudness -14.39 LUFS is significantly louder than EBU R128 broadcast target -23.0 LUFS ±0.5 LU. This is typical for music streaming but non-compliant for broadcast.
LRA of 3.41 LU is below typical broadcast range of 5-20 LU, indicating highly compressed dynamics. This is common for electronic dance music but may lack dynamic variation.
True peak -0.84 dBTP exceeds EBU R128 maximum of -1.0 dBTP. Risk of inter-sample peaks and clipping on some playback systems.
Maximum momentary loudness -13.2 LUFS exceeds EBU R128 typical maximum of -18.0 LUFS (gate-dependent).
Maximum short-term loudness -14.38 LUFS exceeds EBU R128 typical maximum of -20.0 LUFS (gate-dependent).

K-weighted loudness -14.39 LUFS (model-estimated by masteringbrainv2; the SonicMaster path peak-normalizes input to -1 dBFS before inference, so this is the model's target-derived estimate, NOT an ITU-R BS.1770-4 measurement of the input waveform). For a traceable BS.1770-4 measurement use the AutoMasteringEngine LUFS meter (getLUFSIntegrated).

True peak -0.84 dBTP (decoded from the model's limiter-ceiling output; the SonicMaster analysis path measures sample-peak only, not 4x-oversampled true peak. For a BS.1770-4 true-peak reading use AutoMasteringEngine::getTruePeak_dBTP). Exceeds -1.0 dBTP limit.
LRA (3.41 LU) and dynamic range measurement (12.51 dB) show inconsistency >3 LU tolerance. LRA indicates high compression while dynamic range suggests moderate compression.
Crest factor 15.17 dB indicates good instantaneous dynamics, consistent with low compression detection.
THD value of 11.3% is consistent between harmonic module (11.3%) and canonical_measurements (11.3%)
THD of 11.3% exceeds creative_distortion threshold (0.1%) but may be intentional for genre; verify artistic intent
Fundamental frequency (72.604 Hz) does not match detected key C# minor (expected ~138.591-155.563 Hz for C#3-D3 range). Saturation_profile indicates 'No clear fundamental detected'
H2 level of -14.91 dB falls within typical -6 to -20 dB range for tube saturation
H3 level of -26.68 dB falls within typical -12 to -30 dB range
Even-odd ratio of 3.775 indicates even-order dominance consistent with tube saturation
Harmonic profile shows H2 strongest (-14.91 dB) but H6 (-31.221 dB) is stronger than H4 (-39.74 dB) and H5 (-44.615 dB), suggesting complex saturation or measurement artifact
Nyquist frequency is 22050 Hz; harmonic profile shows no harmonics near this limit (H8 at ~580 Hz assuming fundamental ~72.6 Hz)
Key confidence of 0.296 is low; chroma vector shows C# (0.917) as strongest but fundamental frequency doesn't align with C# minor
Saturation type 'tube' matches even-odd ratio of 3.775 (even-order dominant) and harmonic profile
Spectral centroid (1460.65 Hz) is significantly below the Melodic Techno reference range of 2000-3500 Hz. This indicates the mix is too bass-heavy and lacks high-frequency energy.

correction.reasoning: Bring spectral centroid into genre-typical range by reducing low frequencies and boosting presence/air bands.

findings.message: Spectral rolloff (7298.53 Hz) is within plausible range for electronic music.
Spectral tilt (-3.35 dB/octave) is within typical range. Frequency-enhanced measurement shows -7.15 dB/octave, indicating some measurement variation but both are plausible.
Band energy distribution sums to 0.9995 (within ±1.5dB tolerance of 1.0).
Low frequency balance (0.842) is severely excessive compared to Melodic Techno reference (0.3-0.42). This confirms the bass-heavy spectral imbalance.

correction.reasoning: Reduce low frequency energy by approximately 10-12 dB to match genre standards.

findings.message: Primary resonance peak at 13454 Hz is within audible range but has unusually wide bandwidth (11211.7 Hz) for Q=1.2. This suggests measurement artifact rather than true resonance.

correction.reasoning: Verify resonance detection with alternative algorithm; consider ignoring this peak if not audible.

findings.message: Transient-harmonic ratio (0.54) is consistent with spectral flatness (0.01) indicating predominantly harmonic content.
EQ reconstruction fit quality (mean band confidence 0.786) is acceptable.
Detected BPM (61.2) is far outside expected Melodic Techno range (120±15 BPM). Likely half-time detection error or tempo drift issue.
Beat regularity (0.5) is very low, inconsistent with BPM confidence (0.689). Indicates significant tempo instability or detection errors.
Transient density (5.967) aligns with onset count (2395) over 401s duration. Ratio is plausible.
Attack time (21.4ms) is within typical instrument range. Consistent with low percussive content (0.039).
Tempo stability (0.058) is extremely low. Confirmed by tempo_variations showing 61.5, 80.7, 97.5 BPM fluctuations.
Beat count is 0, indicating complete failure of beat tracking. No phase alignment possible.
BPM (61.2) is within physical limits, but likely represents half-time of actual tempo.
Canonical LUFS (-14.39) differs from arrangement_dynamics integrated loudness (-16.8). Using canonical value as authoritative.

predicted_issues: Codec THD contribution (2.0%) pushes total THD above acceptable mastering threshold
Codec THD contribution (30.0%) pushes total THD above acceptable mastering threshold
Codec THD contribution (5.0%) pushes total THD above acceptable mastering threshold
Codec THD contribution (2.0%) pushes total THD above acceptable mastering threshold

master_bus_chain.reason: Apply input trim to achieve target headroom and align with Apple Music +1.61 dB requirement
Remove excessive bass, mud, boxiness, and nasal frequencies to correct spectral imbalance
Control dynamics per band to tighten low end and enhance clarity while maintaining crest factor
Reduce THD from 11.3% to warm mixing level while maintaining tube character
Boost presence and air bands to improve spectral centroid and brightness for Melodic Techno
Increase stereo width from 0.041 to enhance spatial immersion while maintaining mono compatibility
Set ceiling to -1.5 dBTP to comply with Amazon Music -2.0 dBTP and prevent inter-sample peaks
Apply dither for 16-bit delivery to reduce quantization noise

cross_validations.message: Frequency and harmonic analyses are internally consistent
Dynamic range and transient detection are consistent
Stereo imaging and tonal character are consistent
Dynamics and frequency measurements are mutually supportive
Harmonic distortion measurements are consistent with source format
Transient and spatial characteristics are consistent
Overall plausibility concern: minimum agent score is 0.20, average is 0.50
cross_validations.resolution: Review lowest-scoring domain for measurement errors

findings.note: AUDIT-FIX-1: value shown is the masteringbrainv2 model estimate, NOT a BS.1770-4 measurement. The SonicMaster path peak-normalizes the input to -1 dBFS before inference, destroying absolute loudness; the model cannot measure input LUFS. For a true BS.1770-4 K-weighted dual-gate measurement use AutoMasteringEngine::getLUFSIntegrated().

overall_assessment.summary: Severe spectral imbalance with excessive low-frequency energy (84.2% vs 30% benchmark) and deficient mids/highs, requiring aggressive low-end reduction and significant presence/air enhancement to achieve Melodic Techno tonal balance. Mix is extremely narrow and centered with excellent mono compatibility but lacks the wide atmospheric stereo image expected for Melodic Techno. Needs significant mid-high frequency expansion while maintaining mono bass foundation.

stereo_adjustments.width_recommendation: Mix is extremely narrow (0.041 width, 0.976 correlation). Need significant stereo expansion in mid-high frequencies while keeping bass mono. Target 0.4-0.6 width for Melodic Techno atmospheric feel.

system_translation.car_audio: Good translation with balanced frequency response. Narrow stereo image may reduce spatial interest in car environment.
system_translation.headphones: Will translate well but will sound overly centered/narrow. Needs stereo expansion for proper headphone imaging.
system_translation.club_speakers: Good translation with strong bass correlation (1.0) but lacks stereo width for immersive club experience. Mono compatibility is excellent.
system_translation.phone_speakers: Acceptable but bass-heavy mix may lose low-end on small speakers. Narrow stereo not an issue for mono phone playback.
